attributes.description: A scalar or vector field defined on a manifold. Field instances always reference a manifold (via manifold_ref). The schema describes the data slot (values) plus the operations that can be performed on the field: gradient, divergence, isolines, streamlines, Hodge decomposition, and Poisson solve. Internal heat-method machinery (vector heat, signed heat, heat diffusion) lives under-the-hood and is not exposed as a field.
attributes.manifold_ref: <URI to the manifold this field lives on, e.g. ./manifold.schema.json>
attributes.units: field-specific (e.g. 'mV' for cortical activity, 'm/s' for velocity)
attributes.binding_storage_rule: scalar fields flatten as [D] float64 row-major (D = V|E|F per domain); vector fields flatten as [D, 3] row-major. Time-varying fields use [T, D] (or [T, D, 3]) frame-major float32 — matching the activity time-series convention in include/nxr/compute.h.

attributes.description: The field's data buffer. Shape and memory size depend on (kind, domain): scalar+vertex = [V] (the common case for activity / curvature / scalar potentials); scalar+edge = [E] (1-form); scalar+face = [F]; vector+vertex = [V, 3]; vector+face = [F, 3] (face tangent vector field); vector+edge = [E, 3] (rare). The 'shape' attribute below uses 'D' as the polymorphic domain count.
attributes.shape: [D] (scalar) | [D, 3] (vector)   where D ∈ {V, E, F} per attributes.domain
attributes.memory_bytes: 8 * D (scalar) | 8 * D * 3 (vector)   where D ∈ {V, E, F}
attributes.formula: user-supplied (filled by consumer from measurement, simulation, generator, or another operation's output)

attributes.description: Gradient of a scalar vertex field, returned as per-face 3D vectors via Whitney interpolation of d0(values). Equivalent to applying the manifold's gradient operator (d0) and lifting to face centroids.

output.description: per-face gradient vectors at face centroids

attributes.formula: gradient(values) on face f = (1 / 2 A_f) Σ (values_k - values_i) (n × e_ji)

attributes.description: Divergence of a vector or 1-form field, returned as a per-vertex scalar. For a face-vector field, the binding internally projects to an edge 1-form and applies operators/divergence (= d0^T * hodge1). For an edge 1-form input, applies operators/divergence directly.
attributes.formula: divergence(values) = d0^T * hodge1 * lift(values)   where lift converts face-vectors to a 1-form when kind=vector

attributes.description: Extract isolines (contour polylines) of a scalar vertex field at numLevels evenly-spaced contour values in [minValue, maxValue]. If minValue == maxValue, the binding auto-detects the range from the data.

inputs.name: min_value
inputs.description: lower bound of contour range; pass max_value=min_value to auto-detect

output.description: polyline endpoint pairs; N is the total segment count across all levels

implemented_by.mex: nxr_compute('computeIsolines', V, F, values, num_levels, min_value, max_value)

attributes.description: Trace quasi-uniform streamlines through a face vector field via forward Euler with face-crossing detection.

inputs.name: step_coef
inputs.description: step length in units of mean edge length

output.description: polyline segment endpoint pairs; N is the total segment count

implemented_by.mex: nxr_compute('traceStreamlines', V, F, values, num_seeds, step_coef, max_steps)

attributes.description: Hodge / Helmholtz decomposition of an edge 1-form ω: ω = dα + δβ + γ. Returns the three scalar potentials (α at vertices, β at faces, γ harmonic on edges) plus their face-centered vector lifts.

output.shape: {exact_potential[V], co_exact_potential_v[V], combined_potential[V], d_alpha[E], delta_beta[E], gamma[E], omega_vectors[F,3], d_alpha_vectors[F,3], delta_beta_vectors[F,3], gamma_vectors[F,3]}
output.description: scalar potentials + 1-form components + face-centered vector lifts

attributes.formula: values = d0 * α + hodge1_inverse * d1^T * β + γ   where γ is harmonic (d* γ = d γ = 0)

attributes.description: Solve the scalar Poisson equation L φ = -M (values - mean(values)) on the manifold. Returns a per-vertex harmonic potential φ. Reuses the manifold's CholeskyCache for the (L + ε I) factor — repeated calls amortise the factorisation cost.
